description: Lorentzian 3+1 causal-ordering layer of the emergent-spacetime construction. The relational lattice carries (i) a clean causal partial order on Xi-paths, (ii) a 3+1 spatial-temporal decomposition with d_spatial=3 + 1 emergent time direction (d_spacetime=4), (iii) an existence statement for a Lorentzian metric in both regimes, and (iv) a discrete light-cone-fuzziness scalar that quantifies the residual deviation of the lattice light-cone from a perfect Minkowski cone. The light-cone-fuzziness is identified with the spectral-dimension residual d_eff_residual = d_eff - 3.0 in both regimes, which gives the 3+1-aware metric-quality observable that is the right Riemannian-asymptotics complement to the strictly spatial Kruskal-Shepard MDS-stress sigma(N). Distilled from the companion emergent-time bundle covering causal ordering, arrow of time, proper time, thermodynamic time, and an aggregate assessment.

causal_ordering.interpretation: Massless transport modes -> signal propagation at maximum speed -> defines light cone -> defines causal structure.

metric_quality_axes_summary.description: Three different metric-quality observables, all bundled in this companion package. They probe complementary facets of the discrete-to-continuum bridge.

axes.name: Riemannian-embedding stress (spatial only)
axes.scope: Kruskal-Shepard MDS in 3-D Euclidean target dimension; signature-blind; bottoms out at sigma_inf ~ 0.30 under two-point Richardson, i.e. does NOT vanish at the available lattice sizes.

axes.name: Lorentzian light-cone fuzziness (3+1 causal-ordering)
axes.scope: Spectral-dimension residual of the 3+1 emergent-spacetime layer; the right 3+1-aware metric-quality complement to sigma(N).

axes.name: Einstein-identity gap
axes.scope: Direct test of the Einstein equations on the Lorentzian 3+1 manifold; converges under two-point Richardson at alpha = 0.848 to gap_inf ~ 1e-6.

differences.interpretation: sigma is roughly the sum of (i) a 3-D vs 3+1 dimension/signature-mismatch contribution, captured at canonical regime by sigma - f_LC ~ 0.11, and (ii) the 3+1-Lorentzian intrinsic light-cone fuzziness f_LC ~ 0.17. The Einstein-identity gap is in the same range as f_LC and converges, while sigma does not.

framing: The Lorentzian 3+1 causal-ordering layer is the load-bearing prerequisite for both the Einstein-identity-gap closure (Schwarzschild far-field, PPN, Jacobson area-law) AND the black-hole-sector results (Bekenstein-Hawking 1/4, Kerr structure, Penrose process, Peters binary inspiral, information-paradox unitarity). The narrow Einstein-identity-gap and the broader Riemannian-embedding-stress are complementary diagnostics; the light-cone fuzziness sits between them and quantifies the 3+1-aware metric-quality residual.
